Section.name: UNCLASSIFIED
Section.Content: Prepared by: Johan Pauwels Date: 13/03/2024 Cegeka reference: PRD.4.2.001 DV1.1 CONFIDENTIAL Service Description PRD.4.2.001 Managed Dedicated FW platform Table of contents 1.1 Service Description 4 1.2 Service identification 4 1.3 Service summary 4 1.3.1 Infrastructure management 4 1.3.2 The Infrastructure 5 1.4 Service features 5 1.4.1 Standard Service features 5 1.4.1.1 Infrastructure knowledge 5 1.4.1.2 Technical setup 5 1.4.1.3 Technical management 5 1.4.1.4 Housekeeping 6 1.4.1.5 Monitoring 7 1.4.1.6 Security and Compliance 8 1.4.1.7 Backup and Restore 8 1.4.1.8 Incident and problem management 8 1.4.1.9 Customers responsibility 8 1.4.2 Optional Service features 8 1.4.3 Requests 9 1.4.4 Out of scope 9 1.5 Service characteristics 10 1.5.1 Obligatory 10 1.5.2 Cegeka owned Licenses and/or subscriptions 10 1.5.3 Customer owned Licenses and/or subscriptions 10 1.5.4 Conditions 11 1.5.5 Service delivery location 11 1.5.6 Service Reporting 11 1.5.7 Data classification 11 1.5.8 Use of Subcontractor/Sub-outsourcer 11 1.5.9 Miscellaneous 11 1.5.10 RACI Matrix 12 1.6 Adherence to Cross-Functional Services 14 2 Service Level Agreement 15 2.1 Service Window 15 2.2 Service Level 15 3 Service Billing 16 3.1 Standard Service Billing 16 3.2 Optional Service Billing 16 3.3 Change Request Billing 16 4 Document control 17 5 Document approval 18

Section.name: Service identification
Section.Content: Service Managed Dedicated Firewall Platform Unique ID PRD.4.2.001 Service Model Infrastructure management (IaaS)

Section.name: Infrastructure management
Section.Content: This Service is the management and administration of a dedicated FW platform for the Customer (the Infrastructure). Important note: The dedicated firewall platforms for Argenta are only used for Cegeka service delivery & connectivity. Firewalling for the Argenta servers and from/to the external area is managed by a 3 rd party. As a result, this service description’s RACI leaves out the Customer’s A and R roles that would be in place when the dedicated firewall platforms were also used for the Customer systems. A FW platform can be both physical or virtual: The Service includes the deployment, management, maintenance, and monitoring. The Standard Service features are focussed to keep the Service up-and-running, to keep it secure and to optimize its technical parameters.

Section.name: The Infrastructure
Section.Content: The FW platform can be of different makes and models. Each dedicated FW platform will be evaluated first by Cegeka and approved to take into management. Leading firewalls are covered. General characteristics (depending on the make/model) : Cegeka can provide redundant setup in 2 Cegeka DC locations for HA/DR configurations. Hardware, licenses and maintenance contracts to be acquired by the Customer. Cegeka can provide offers for such items.

Section.name: Standard Service features
Section.Content: Cegeka ensures skilled engineers with experience on the infrastructure in order to perform the Service. Cegeka proposes the setup of the Infrastructure in order to set it up according to the technology requirements of the Infrastructure and in line with the best practices of the vendor. The Customer is consulted in the setup. Technical setup is documented on operational level and is available to the Customer. At end-of-life, the Customer is responsible for the removal and desctruction of physical devices. Cegeka is responsible for the technical operation of the Infrastructure and to assure it’s correct running during the agreed availability windows. Recurrent maintenance activities (housekeeping) are needed to ensure the correct working of the Infrastructure. Automation is applied where possible. Proactive management Housekeeping includes following regular checks: Resolution actions are triggered when needed and followed to assure the SLA. Patching The service includes a maximum of 2 patch cycles (per environment) per year for the managed service. Patching is done in accordance to the operational Patch Plan and in line with the vendors best practice. Life Cycle Management Cegeka respects the operating system lifecycle as provided by the vendor. Cegeka monitors the Infrastructure and notifies the Customer timely when they are subject to end-of-life. This includes, if applicable, the extension or replacement of the Infrastructure. Cegeka also notifies the Customer upon newer versions of the Infrastructure in order to plan the update of the platform in time within the followed (relative) version (for example N-1). Life Cycle Updates will be planned in accordance with the agreed maintenance windows and will be executed on T&M basis. The customer will be informed at least 6 months in advance to properly plan the upgrade, as significant customer effort is required in application testing. Cegeka monitors the correct working of the Infrastructure. Monitoring improvements will be done during the lifecycle of the Service. Monitoring events are evaluated during the Service Window in accordance to the Service Level of the individual environment. Notable events are treated as Incident tickets and are handled according to regular incident management. Management of technical users The management of technical users (for engineers) is done by Cegeka in agreement with the Customer and in accordance to the Customer IAM policies. Detection of security incidents The security management is performed following the Cegeka internal security policies and the contractual Customer security policies. Security incidents are created in case a security breach is detected and is immediately communicated to the Customer and Cegeka stakeholders. Application logs for SIEM purposes or compliance purposes Additional monitoring can be done based on System and/or Application logs, which can be forwarded to a Customer SIEM solution (Security reason) or other solution (compliance reason, e.g. central collection of application logs to oversee the application stack) on request. The operational Backup Plan contains the information on frequency and retention of the backups in accordance to the Customer backup policy. Duration of a restore is dependant of the request (file or full; size of the item(s) to be restored. Besides monitoring incidents (see ‘Monitoring’) also other incidents can be reported by both the Customer and Cegeka. These will be subject to the regular incident management process (See Cross-functionals). Related to incident management, Cegeka performs also problem management (See Cross-functionals). Optional, the Customer may have administrative access to the environment(s) to be able to manage their configurations.

Section.name: Optional Service features
Section.Content: This section describes the features that can be optionally added to the standard Service features and that will be priced (when applicable) on top of the standard Service features. The following optional features are possible with the Service:

Section.name: Requests
Section.Content: Both the Customer and Cegeka can issue a Request for Change (RFC) or a Request for Information (RFI). There are multiple types of requests: The handling of Requests is subject to the regular change management process (see Cross-functionals). request type Description Lead time Service Requests Add/remove/change firewall rules/setup 3 working days Normal Changes Any other request. Indicated case-by-case during the analysis of the request.

Section.name: Out of scope
Section.Content: Everything that is not explicitly mentioned in this service description is by default out of scope of this Service.

Section.name: Obligatory
Section.Content: The following services are mandatory for the delivery of this service:

Section.name: Cegeka owned Licenses and/or subscriptions
Section.Content: These licenses and/or subscriptions and their cost are included in this Service. It is Cegeka’s responsibility to maintain license and/or subscription compliance. Licenses and/or subscriptions used for Cross-Functional Services are included in the Service and are not listed below. License included in service applies to n/a n/a

Section.name: Customer owned Licenses and/or subscriptions
Section.Content: These licenses and/or subscriptions and their cost are not included in this Service. It is the customers responsibility to maintain license and/or subscription compliance. License description/remarks Firewall licenses & maintenance contract According to the selected make/models and features needed for the Customers solution. Administration of licenses involves: License compliancy Customer has to share the available licenses with Cegeka. Cegeka will assist in customer license compliance by providing knowledge and reporting on use of licenses and license requirements. Cegeka ensures to implement or activate features that are only in-line with the licenses owned by the customer.

Section.name: Conditions
Section.Content: A support contract for the FW to be managed needs to be in place by the Customer, where Cegeka must have the possibility to use.

Section.name: Service delivery location
Section.Content: This Service is delivered remotely from the Cegeka (virtual) Office environment.

Section.name: Service Reporting
Section.Content: Monthly reporting will be provided to inform on the availability and meeting the SLA’s and to provide insights on the capacity and expected extensions required.

Section.name: Data classification
Section.Content: Cegeka requires no access to confidential and/or personal data to perform this Service. Engineer credentials do not need to access such data for the purpose of this Service. In case of assistance in investigations of functional issues, Cegeka personnel may see or receive fragments of such data in case it is shared by the Customer (data fragments in the sense of exports, screen sharing, screen shots, etc. for investigation purposes).

Section.name: Use of Subcontractor/Sub-outsourcer
Section.Content: This service is delivered with: Subcontractor (Cegeka manages the Service with own engineers which are supported by an external party for escalations or technology specific questions or issues): (none, note: a support contract by the Customer for the FW needs to be in place as stated in the Conditions chapter). Sub-outsourcer (Cegeka delegates the Service to an external party): (none)

Section.name: Miscellaneous
Section.Content: n/a

Section.name: RACI Matrix
Section.Content: Activity Cegeka Customer Technical platform management A,R Change management A,R Executing standard requests A,R Executing non-standard requests A,R Architecture & Design Evaluate & Selection Platform (Hardware & Software) A,R Lifecycle Management Platform (Hardware & Software) A,R HA/DR/Backup requirements A,R HA/DR/Backup solution, installation and configuration A,R License Management Platform Licensing model A,R Platform Licensing model Compliance A,R Plan & Deliver Platform IaaS, Network & Connectivity (when applicable) A,R Implement Platform Backup A,R Change Management A,R Request Fulfilment A,R Housekeeping (manage & assure) Trigger Platform Technology Refresh (Hardware & Software) (lifecycle management) A,R Platform Operations (Hardware & Software) A,R Firmware Patch Management A,R Monitoring Platform Capacity A,R Platform Monitoring (availability, performance, backup status) & Alerting A,R Operate & Maintain Platform Backup A,R Incident Management (proactive by monitoring) A,R Problem Management (proactive by monitoring) A,R Evaluate and assess patch and upgrade effect on infrastructure A,R Functional testing after deploying patches and upgrades A,R Security Platform Technology security A,R Advise and follow up of security and auditing measures A,R Security Patch Management A,R RACI role definition:

Section.name: Adherence to Cross-Functional Services
Section.Content: The Service described in this Service Description adheres to the Cross-functional Services as described in the contract where this Service is residing unless documented in the table below: Cross-Functional Service Deviation from adherence to cross-functional services n/a The Cross -Functional services rely on the Cegeka standard framework for processes and tooling.

Section.name: Service Window
Section.Content: The Service Window is the time frame during which the agreed support services from Cegeka are available. Service windows are defined in the related Service Level Management document to this contract.

Section.name: Service Level
Section.Content: Service Levels of the cross-functional services are defined in the related Service Level Management document to this contract. Availability The following Availability KPI’s are provided for the Service: KPI ID KPI name Availability Service Window Metric KPI.4.2.001.AVAIL247 Availability 24/7 Monthly: 99,5% Yearly (sliding window): 99,9% The availability measurement is monthly and calculated as:

Section.name: Standard Service Billing
Section.Content: The service is billed on a monthly basis. ID Component Billing element BILLING UNIT SKU.4.2.001.L01 Managed Firewall Managed dedicated Firewall Per FW cluster Measurement The quantity of each billing element is determined on a monthly basis: Calculation / Billing The price(s) is/are determined in the pricing schedule where this Service is residing. The Service is billed per billing element:

Section.name: Optional Service Billing
Section.Content: n/a

Section.name: Change Request Billing
Section.Content: Changes are billed on a monthly basis. Changes as result of Pre-approved and Normal Service requests are billed according to the defined prices in the pricing document of the related contact. Non-standard change requests are billed on T&M basis.

Section.name: Document control
Section.Content: Document version Date Author Notes DV1.0 21/06/2024 Johan Pauwels First version aligned with structural Service Description requirements. DV1.1 22/11/2024 Johan Pauwels Comments removed, document cleanup for PDF.
